   729вҶ’## Cost Analysis
   730вҶ’
   731вҶ’### Monthly Cost Estimate (Production)
   732вҶ’
   733вҶ’| Component | Configuration | Estimated Cost (USD) |
   734вҶ’|-----------|---------------|---------------------|
   735вҶ’| **DynamoDB** | On-demand, ~1000 writes/month, ~5000 reads/month | $2.50 |
   736вҶ’| **DynamoDB Global Tables** | Cross-region replication | $1.25 |
   737вҶ’| **Lambda (DLQ-Consumer)** | 256MB, ~500 invocations/month, 10s avg | $0.05 |
   738вҶ’| **Lambda (Replay)** | 512MB, ~100 invocations/month, 30s avg | $0.02 |
   739вҶ’| **Lambda (Stuck Recovery)** | 256MB, 2880 invocations/month (every 15 min) | $0.10 |
   740вҶ’| **Lambda (TTL Archiver)** | 256MB, ~50 invocations/month | $0.01 |
   741вҶ’| **API Gateway** | ~200 requests/month | $0.70 |
   742вҶ’| **KMS** | 1 key, ~2000 encrypt/decrypt ops | $1.03 |
   743вҶ’| **S3 (Archive)** | ~1GB/year, Glacier after 1 year | $0.01 |
   744вҶ’| **CloudWatch** | Logs, metrics, alarms | $3.00 |
   745вҶ’| **EventBridge** | ~100 events/month | $0.00 |
   746вҶ’| **WAF** | Basic rules, ~200 requests | $5.50 |
   747вҶ’| **VPC Endpoints** | DynamoDB, SQS, KMS (if not existing) | $21.60 |
   748вҶ’| **Total** | | **~$35.77/month** |
   749вҶ’
   750вҶ’### Cost Optimization Strategies
   751вҶ’
   752вҶ’1. **VPC Endpoints Sharing**: Use existing VPC endpoints if available (saves $21.60/month)
   753вҶ’2. **Reserved Concurrency**: Avoid over-provisioning Lambda concurrency
   754вҶ’3. **GSI Projections**: Use INCLUDE instead of ALL to reduce storage
   755вҶ’4. **S3 Lifecycle**: Aggressive transition to Glacier for archives
   756вҶ’5. **Alarm Consolidation**: Use composite alarms to reduce alarm count
   757вҶ’
   758вҶ’### Cost Monitoring
   759вҶ’
   760вҶ’```yaml
   761вҶ’FeeExemptionBudget:
   762вҶ’  Type: AWS::Budgets::Budget
   763вҶ’  Properties:
   764вҶ’    Budget:
   765вҶ’      BudgetName: fee-exemption-monthly
   766вҶ’      BudgetLimit:
   767вҶ’        Amount: 50
   768вҶ’        Unit: USD
   769вҶ’      TimeUnit: MONTHLY
   770вҶ’      BudgetType: COST
   771вҶ’      CostFilters:
   772вҶ’        TagKeyValue:
   773вҶ’          - "user:Application$fee-exemption"
   774вҶ’    NotificationsWithSubscribers:
   775вҶ’      - Notification:
   776вҶ’          NotificationType: ACTUAL
   777вҶ’          ComparisonOperator: GREATER_THAN
   778вҶ’          Threshold: 80
   779вҶ’        Subscribers:
   780вҶ’          - SubscriptionType: EMAIL
   781вҶ’            Address: squad-fenix@company.com
   782вҶ’```

   794вҶ’### Version Lifecycle

   796вҶ’| Version | Status | End of Life |
   797вҶ’|---------|--------|-------------|
   798вҶ’| v1 | **Current** | - |
   799вҶ’| v2 (future) | Planned | - |
   800вҶ’
   801вҶ’### Breaking Change Policy
   802вҶ’
   803вҶ’1. **Non-breaking changes**: Add to current version
   804вҶ’   - New optional fields in responses
   805вҶ’   - New optional query parameters
   806вҶ’   - New endpoints
   807вҶ’
   808вҶ’2. **Breaking changes**: New major version
   809вҶ’   - Removing fields
   810вҶ’   - Changing field types
   811вҶ’   - Changing URL structure
   812вҶ’

  1005вҶ’## Multi-Environment Strategy
  1006вҶ’
  1007вҶ’### Environment Matrix
  1008вҶ’
  1009вҶ’| Environment | Purpose | Data | CBILL Connection |
  1010вҶ’|-------------|---------|------|------------------|
  1011вҶ’| **dev** | Development & debugging | Synthetic | Mock server |
  1012вҶ’| **staging** | Integration testing | Anonymized prod copy | Staging CBILL |
  1013вҶ’| **prod** | Production | Real | Production CBILL |
  1014вҶ’
  1015вҶ’### Environment Parity
  1016вҶ’
  1017вҶ’```yaml
  1018вҶ’# All environments use same CloudFormation templates
  1019вҶ’Parameters:
  1020вҶ’  Environment:
  1021вҶ’    Type: String
  1022вҶ’    AllowedValues: [dev, staging, prod]
  1023вҶ’
  1024вҶ’Mappings:
  1025вҶ’  EnvironmentConfig:
  1026вҶ’    dev:
  1027вҶ’      DynamoDBBillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
  1028вҶ’      LambdaMemory: 256
  1029вҶ’      CircuitBreakerEnabled: "false"
  1030вҶ’      LogLevel: DEBUG
  1031вҶ’    staging:
  1032вҶ’      DynamoDBBillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
  1033вҶ’      LambdaMemory: 256
  1034вҶ’      CircuitBreakerEnabled: "true"
  1035вҶ’      LogLevel: INFO
  1036вҶ’    prod:
  1037вҶ’      DynamoDBBillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
  1038вҶ’      LambdaMemory: 256
  1039вҶ’      CircuitBreakerEnabled: "true"
  1040вҶ’      LogLevel: INFO
  1041вҶ’```
  1042вҶ’
  1043вҶ’### Data Handling by Environment
  1044вҶ’
  1045вҶ’```typescript
  1046вҶ’// Environment-specific CPF handling
  1047вҶ’const getCpfSalt = async (): Promise<string> => {
  1048вҶ’  const env = process.env.ENVIRONMENT;
  1049вҶ’
  1050вҶ’  if (env === 'dev') {
  1051вҶ’    return 'dev-static-salt';  // Deterministic for debugging
  1052вҶ’  }
  1053вҶ’
  1054вҶ’  return ssmClient.getParameter({
  1055вҶ’    Name: `/fee-exemption/${env}/salt`,
  1056вҶ’    WithDecryption: true
  1057вҶ’  });
  1058вҶ’};
  1059вҶ’
  1060вҶ’// Test data factory (dev/staging only)
  1061вҶ’const generateTestCpf = (): string => {
  1062вҶ’  if (process.env.ENVIRONMENT === 'prod') {
  1063вҶ’    throw new Error('Cannot generate test CPFs in production');
  1064вҶ’  }
  1065вҶ’
  1066вҶ’  // Generate valid CPF for testing
  1067вҶ’  return generateValidCpf();
  1068вҶ’};
  1069вҶ’```

  1073вҶ’## Migration Runbook
  1074вҶ’
  1075вҶ’### Pre-Migration Checklist
  1076вҶ’
  1077вҶ’- [ ] FeeExemption Lambda idempotency implemented and tested
  1078вҶ’- [ ] KMS key created with correct policies
  1079вҶ’- [ ] VPC endpoints configured (if required)
  1080вҶ’- [ ] IAM roles created and tested
  1081вҶ’- [ ] CloudWatch alarms configured
  1082вҶ’- [ ] Runbooks written and reviewed
  1083вҶ’- [ ] Team trained on new workflow
  1084вҶ’- [ ] Rollback plan tested in staging
  1085вҶ’
  1086вҶ’### Migration Steps
  1087вҶ’
  1088вҶ’#### Phase 0: Prerequisites (Week 1)
  1089вҶ’
  1090вҶ’```bash
  1091вҶ’# 1. Deploy KMS key
  1092вҶ’aws cloudformation deploy \
  1093вҶ’  --template-file infrastructure/kms.yml \
  1094вҶ’  --stack-name fee-exemption-kms-prod \
  1095вҶ’  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod
  1096вҶ’
  1097вҶ’# 2. Deploy DynamoDB table (no consumers yet)
  1098вҶ’aws cloudformation deploy \
  1099вҶ’  --template-file infrastructure/dynamodb.yml \
  1100вҶ’  --stack-name fee-exemption-dynamodb-prod \
  1101вҶ’  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod
  1102вҶ’
  1103вҶ’# 3. Verify FeeExemption Lambda has idempotency
  1104вҶ’aws lambda invoke \
  1105вҶ’  --function-name NTS-APP-Fintech-FeeExemption \
  1106вҶ’  --payload '{"test": true, "idempotency_key": "test-key"}' \
  1107вҶ’  response.json
  1108вҶ’
  1109вҶ’# 4. Create SSM parameters
  1110вҶ’aws ssm put-parameter \
  1111вҶ’  --name /fee-exemption/prod/salt \
  1112вҶ’  --type SecureString \
  1113вҶ’  --value "$(openssl rand -base64 32)"
  1114вҶ’```
  1115вҶ’
  1116вҶ’#### Phase 1: Canary Deployment (Week 2)
  1117вҶ’
  1118вҶ’```bash
  1119вҶ’# 1. Deploy DLQ-Consumer with 1% traffic
  1120вҶ’aws lambda create-event-source-mapping \
  1121вҶ’  --function-name fee-exemption-dlq-consumer-prod \
  1122вҶ’  --event-source-arn arn:aws:sqs:sa-east-1:123456789012:SQS-Isencao_mensalidade_DLQ \
  1123вҶ’  --batch-size 1 \
  1124вҶ’  --scaling-config MaximumConcurrency=1
  1125вҶ’
  1126вҶ’# 2. Monitor for 48 hours
  1127вҶ’# - Check CloudWatch Logs for errors
  1128вҶ’# - Verify DynamoDB records are created
  1129вҶ’# - Confirm no impact on existing Step Function
  1130вҶ’
  1131вҶ’# 3. If successful, increase to 100%
  1132вҶ’aws lambda update-event-source-mapping \
  1133вҶ’  --uuid <UUID> \
  1134вҶ’  --batch-size 10 \
  1135вҶ’  --scaling-config MaximumConcurrency=5
  1136вҶ’```
  1137вҶ’
  1138вҶ’#### Phase 2: Full Deployment (Week 3)
  1139вҶ’
  1140вҶ’```bash
  1141вҶ’# 1. Deploy Replay Lambda and API Gateway
  1142вҶ’aws cloudformation deploy \
  1143вҶ’  --template-file infrastructure/replay.yml \
  1144вҶ’  --stack-name fee-exemption-replay-prod \
  1145вҶ’  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod
  1146вҶ’
  1147вҶ’# 2. Configure auto-replay schedule
  1148вҶ’aws events put-rule \
  1149вҶ’  --name fee-exemption-auto-replay-prod \
  1150вҶ’  --schedule-expression "cron(0 6 * * ? *)" \
  1151вҶ’  --state DISABLED  # Enable after validation
  1152вҶ’
  1153вҶ’# 3. Deploy stuck recovery Lambda
  1154вҶ’aws cloudformation deploy \
  1155вҶ’  --template-file infrastructure/stuck-recovery.yml \
  1156вҶ’  --stack-name fee-exemption-stuck-recovery-prod
  1157вҶ’
  1158вҶ’# 4. Modify Step Function to use EventBridge trigger
  1159вҶ’# (instead of direct DLQ consumption)
  1160вҶ’```
  1161вҶ’
  1162вҶ’#### Phase 3: Validation (Week 4)
  1163вҶ’
  1164вҶ’```bash
  1165вҶ’# 1. Test manual replay via API
  1166вҶ’curl -X POST https://api.feeexemption.internal/v1/replay/cpf \
  1167вҶ’  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  1168вҶ’  -d '{"cpfs": ["<test-cpf>"]}'
  1169вҶ’
  1170вҶ’# 2. Verify end-to-end flow
  1171вҶ’# - Check DynamoDB record status changed
  1172вҶ’# - Check SQS main queue received message
  1173вҶ’# - Confirm CBILL processed successfully
  1174вҶ’
  1175вҶ’# 3. Enable auto-replay
  1176вҶ’aws events enable-rule --name fee-exemption-auto-replay-prod
  1177вҶ’
  1178вҶ’# 4. Game day exercise
  1179вҶ’# - Simulate CBILL outage
  1180вҶ’# - Verify records persist in DynamoDB
  1181вҶ’# - Verify replay works after recovery
  1182вҶ’```

  1190вҶ’## Operational Runbooks (Updated)
  1191вҶ’
  1192вҶ’### Required Runbooks
  1193вҶ’
  1194вҶ’| ID | Name | Trigger |
  1195вҶ’|----|------|---------|
  1196вҶ’| RB-001 | High Failure Rate Response | > 10 failures/hour |
  1197вҶ’| RB-002 | Stale Records Handling | PENDING_RETRY > 7 days |
  1198вҶ’| RB-003 | Manual CPF Replay | Manual request |
  1199вҶ’| RB-004 | CBILL Outage Response | CBILL unavailable |
  1200вҶ’| RB-005 | Circuit Breaker Open | Auto-replay stopped |
  1201вҶ’| RB-006 | Stuck IN_PROGRESS Recovery | Records > 30 min |
  1202вҶ’| RB-007 | Full System Rollback | Critical failure |
  1203вҶ’| RB-008 | DR Failover | Regional outage (NEW) |
  1204вҶ’| RB-009 | SLO Breach Response | Error budget exhausted (NEW) |
  1205вҶ’| RB-010 | Archive Data Retrieval | Compliance request (NEW) |

  1207вҶ’### RB-009: SLO Breach Response (NEW)
  1208вҶ’
  1209вҶ’```markdown
  1210вҶ’## SLO Breach Response
  1211вҶ’
  1212вҶ’### Trigger
  1213вҶ’- Error budget for any SLO < 10% remaining
  1214вҶ’- CloudWatch Alarm: fee-exemption-slo-breach
  1215вҶ’
  1216вҶ’### Severity Assessment
  1217вҶ’
  1218вҶ’| Budget Remaining | Severity | Response Time |
  1219вҶ’|-----------------|----------|---------------|
  1220вҶ’| < 10% | P2 | 4 hours |
  1221вҶ’| < 5% | P1 | 1 hour |
  1222вҶ’| Exhausted | P0 | Immediate |
  1223вҶ’
  1224вҶ’### Response Steps
  1225вҶ’
  1226вҶ’1. **Identify breaching SLO** (5 min)
  1227вҶ’   - Check CloudWatch Dashboard for affected metric
  1228вҶ’   - Review recent changes (deployments, config changes)
  1229вҶ’
  1230вҶ’2. **Assess impact** (10 min)
  1231вҶ’   - Is the breach ongoing or historical?
  1232вҶ’   - How many customers affected?
  1233вҶ’
  1234вҶ’3. **Mitigation actions by SLO**:
  1235вҶ’
  1236вҶ’   **Availability < 99.9%**:
  1237вҶ’   - Check Lambda error logs
  1238вҶ’   - Verify DynamoDB/SQS health
  1239вҶ’   - Consider DR failover if regional issue
  1240вҶ’
  1241вҶ’   **Durability < 99.999%**:
  1242вҶ’   - Pause DLQ-Consumer if still losing records
  1243вҶ’   - Check for DynamoDB write failures
  1244вҶ’   - Review batch failure handling
  1245вҶ’
  1246вҶ’   **Replay Latency > 5 min (p95)**:
  1247вҶ’   - Check DynamoDB latency metrics
  1248вҶ’   - Review Lambda cold starts
  1249вҶ’   - Consider increasing provisioned concurrency
  1250вҶ’
  1251вҶ’   **Resolution Time > 48h**:
  1252вҶ’   - Investigate stuck records
  1253вҶ’   - Check CBILL availability
  1254вҶ’   - Review error classification accuracy
  1255вҶ’
  1256вҶ’   **Replay Success < 98%**:
  1257вҶ’   - Analyze failure reasons
  1258вҶ’   - Check circuit breaker state
  1259вҶ’   - Review CBILL error responses
  1260вҶ’
  1261вҶ’4. **Document incident** (post-resolution)
  1262вҶ’   - Update incident log
  1263вҶ’   - Schedule post-mortem if P0/P1
  1264вҶ’   - Update runbooks if needed
  1265вҶ’
  1266вҶ’### Escalation
  1267вҶ’
  1268вҶ’| Severity | Escalation Path |
  1269вҶ’|----------|-----------------|
  1270вҶ’| P2 | Squad Fenix on-call |
  1271вҶ’| P1 | Squad Fenix lead + Platform team |
  1272вҶ’| P0 | VP Engineering + All hands |
  1273вҶ’```
  1274вҶ’
  1275вҶ’### RB-010: Archive Data Retrieval (NEW)
  1276вҶ’
  1277вҶ’```markdown
  1278вҶ’## Archive Data Retrieval
  1279вҶ’
  1280вҶ’### Trigger
  1281вҶ’- LGPD data subject request
  1282вҶ’- Legal/compliance inquiry
  1283вҶ’- Audit request
  1284вҶ’
  1285вҶ’### Access Control
  1286вҶ’Only the following roles can request archive retrieval:
  1287вҶ’- Data Protection Officer
  1288вҶ’- Legal Team
  1289вҶ’- Compliance Team
  1290вҶ’
  1291вҶ’### Retrieval Steps
  1292вҶ’
  1293вҶ’1. **Validate request** (1 day SLA)
  1294вҶ’   - Confirm requestor authorization
  1295вҶ’   - Verify CPF format
  1296вҶ’   - Log request for audit trail
  1297вҶ’
  1298вҶ’2. **Determine data location**
  1299вҶ’   - If < 90 days: Query DynamoDB directly
  1300вҶ’   - If 90-365 days: Query S3 Standard/IA
  1301вҶ’   - If > 365 days: Initiate Glacier restore
  1302вҶ’
  1303вҶ’3. **Glacier retrieval** (if applicable)
  1304вҶ’   ```bash
  1305вҶ’   # Initiate restore (Expedited: 1-5 min, Standard: 3-5 hours)
  1306вҶ’   aws s3api restore-object \
  1307вҶ’     --bucket fee-exemption-archive-123456789012-prod \
  1308вҶ’     --key "year=2025/month=01/cpf_token=abc123.parquet" \
  1309вҶ’     --restore-request '{"Days": 7, "GlacierJobParameters": {"Tier": "Standard"}}'
  1310вҶ’   ```
  1311вҶ’
  1312вҶ’4. **Extract requested data**
  1313вҶ’   ```bash
  1314вҶ’   # Query with Athena (S3)
  1315вҶ’   aws athena start-query-execution \
  1316вҶ’     --query-string "SELECT * FROM fee_exemption_archive WHERE cpf_token = 'abc123'" \
  1317вҶ’     --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://query-results/
  1318вҶ’   ```
  1319вҶ’
  1320вҶ’5. **Prepare response**
  1321вҶ’   - Decrypt CPF using KMS
  1322вҶ’   - Format according to LGPD requirements
  1323вҶ’   - Send via secure channel (never email)
  1324вҶ’
  1325вҶ’6. **Log completion**
  1326вҶ’   - Record retrieval in audit log
  1327вҶ’   - Notify requestor
  1328вҶ’   - Delete temporary files
  1329вҶ’```
  1330вҶ’

  1388вҶ’## Appendix: Decision Log
  1389вҶ’
  1390вҶ’| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered |
  1391вҶ’|------|----------|-----------|------------------------|
  1392вҶ’| 2026-02-03 | Use DynamoDB over RDS | Serverless scaling, no connection pooling needed | Aurora Serverless (higher cost) |
  1393вҶ’| 2026-02-03 | CPF tokenization over encryption-only | Faster lookups without decryption | Field-level encryption (slower queries) |
  1394вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | Global Tables for DR | Automatic replication, <1min RPO | Manual cross-region backup (higher RPO) |
  1395вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | S3 for archival over DynamoDB | Cost-effective long-term storage | DynamoDB (10x cost for cold data) |
  1396вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | URI versioning over header | Simpler debugging, cache-friendly | Accept header (more RESTful but complex) |
  1397вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | Parquet for archives | Columnar format for efficient queries | JSON (simpler but larger) |

    17вҶ’## Executive Summary
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’V4 addresses **critical production gaps** identified in V3 reviews (Security: 7.5/10, SRE: 7.5/10, Cost: 7.5/10). This revision focuses on:
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’1. **KMS Security Hardening**: MFA enforcement, key policy restrictions, cross-region key strategy
    22вҶ’2. **Circuit Breaker Persistence**: DynamoDB-backed state machine replacing stateless Lambda + SSM
    23вҶ’3. **TTL Archiver Resilience**: Failure handling with DLQ and retry, preventing data loss
    24вҶ’4. **DR Security Parity**: Explicit IAM, VPC, and KMS configuration for secondary region
    25вҶ’5. **Salt Versioning**: `v1:hash` format with version tracking and migration path
    26вҶ’6. **Architecture Simplification**: Remove over-engineering for ~500 records/month volume
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’### Target Review Score: 9.0/10
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’| Previous Score | Target | Key Improvements |
    31вҶ’|----------------|--------|------------------|
    32вҶ’| 7.5/10 | 9.0/10 | Fix all "Must Fix" items, simplify over-engineered components |
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’---
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’## Critical Issues Addressed
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’### Issue 1: KMS Key Policy Root Access (Security)
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’**Problem**: V3 uses `kms:*` to root account, bypassing all restrictions.
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’**Solution**: Explicit key policy with MFA requirement and least-privilege grants.
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’```yaml
    45вҶ’FeeExemptionKMSKey:
    46вҶ’  Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    47вҶ’  Properties:
    48вҶ’    Description: Encrypts CPF data for fee exemption failures
    49вҶ’    EnableKeyRotation: true
    50вҶ’    PendingWindowInDays: 30
    51вҶ’    MultiRegion: true  # For DR cross-region access
    52вҶ’    KeyPolicy:
    53вҶ’      Version: '2012-10-17'
    54вҶ’      Id: fee-exemption-key-policy
    55вҶ’      Statement:
    56вҶ’        # Key administrators (requires MFA)
    57вҶ’        - Sid: AllowKeyAdministration
    58вҶ’          Effect: Allow
    59вҶ’          Principal:
    60вҶ’            AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/KeyAdminRole'
    61вҶ’          Action:
    62вҶ’            - kms:Create*
    63вҶ’            - kms:Describe*
    64вҶ’            - kms:Enable*
    65вҶ’            - kms:List*
    66вҶ’            - kms:Put*
    67вҶ’            - kms:Update*
    68вҶ’            - kms:Revoke*
    69вҶ’            - kms:Disable*
    70вҶ’            - kms:Get*
    71вҶ’            - kms:Delete*
    72вҶ’            - kms:ScheduleKeyDeletion
    73вҶ’            - kms:CancelKeyDeletion
    74вҶ’          Resource: '*'
    75вҶ’          Condition:
    76вҶ’            Bool:
    77вҶ’              'aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent': 'true'
    78вҶ’            NumericLessThan:
    79вҶ’              'aws:MultiFactorAuthAge': '3600'
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’        # Lambda execution roles (encrypt/decrypt only)
    82вҶ’        - Sid: AllowLambdaUsage
    83вҶ’          Effect: Allow
    84вҶ’          Principal:
    85вҶ’            AWS:
    86вҶ’              - !GetAtt DLQConsumerRole.Arn
    87вҶ’              - !GetAtt ReplayLambdaRole.Arn
    88вҶ’              - !GetAtt TTLArchiverRole.Arn
    89вҶ’          Action:
    90вҶ’            - kms:Encrypt
    91вҶ’            - kms:Decrypt
    92вҶ’            - kms:GenerateDataKey
    93вҶ’          Resource: '*'
    94вҶ’          Condition:
    95вҶ’            StringEquals:
    96вҶ’              'kms:ViaService': !Sub 'dynamodb.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com'
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’        # Deny dangerous operations without MFA
    99вҶ’        - Sid: DenyKeyDeletionWithoutMFA
   100вҶ’          Effect: Deny
   101вҶ’          Principal: '*'
   102вҶ’          Action:
   103вҶ’            - kms:ScheduleKeyDeletion
   104вҶ’            - kms:Delete*
   105вҶ’          Resource: '*'
   106вҶ’          Condition:
   107вҶ’            Bool:
   108вҶ’              'aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent': 'false'
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’        # CRITICAL: Deny root account direct access (prevents bypass)
   111вҶ’        - Sid: DenyRootAccountDirectAccess
   112вҶ’          Effect: Deny
   113вҶ’          Principal:
   114вҶ’            AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
   115вҶ’          Action:
   116вҶ’            - kms:Encrypt
   117вҶ’            - kms:Decrypt
   118вҶ’            - kms:GenerateDataKey*
   119вҶ’          Resource: '*'
   120вҶ’          Condition:
   121вҶ’            StringNotLike:
   122вҶ’              'aws:PrincipalArn':
   123вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/KeyAdminRole'
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’        # Cross-region replica access (for DR)
   126вҶ’        - Sid: AllowCrossRegionReplication
   127вҶ’          Effect: Allow
   128вҶ’          Principal:
   129вҶ’            Service: dynamodb.amazonaws.com
   130вҶ’          Action:
   131вҶ’            - kms:Encrypt
   132вҶ’            - kms:Decrypt
   133вҶ’            - kms:GenerateDataKey
   134вҶ’          Resource: '*'
   135вҶ’          Condition:
   136вҶ’            StringEquals:
   137вҶ’              'aws:SourceAccount': !Ref AWS::AccountId
   138вҶ’    Tags:
   139вҶ’      - Key: Application
   140вҶ’        Value: fee-exemption
   141вҶ’      - Key: DataClassification
   142вҶ’        Value: PII
   143вҶ’```
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’### Issue 2: DR Lambda Security Gap (Security/SRE)
   146вҶ’
   147вҶ’**Problem**: V3 secondary region Lambdas lack explicit IAM, VPC, and cross-region KMS config.
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’**Solution**: Complete DR Lambda configuration with security parity.
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’```yaml
   152вҶ’# Secondary Region Lambda (us-east-1) - Complete Configuration
   153вҶ’DRReplayLambda:
   154вҶ’  Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
   155вҶ’  Condition: IsSecondaryRegion
   156вҶ’  Properties:
   157вҶ’    FunctionName: !Sub 'fee-exemption-replay-${Environment}-dr'
   158вҶ’    Runtime: nodejs20.x
   159вҶ’    Handler: index.handler
   160вҶ’    MemorySize: 512
   161вҶ’    Timeout: 300
   162вҶ’    ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’    # VPC Configuration - MUST match primary region security posture
   165вҶ’    VpcConfig:
   166вҶ’      SubnetIds:
   167вҶ’        - !Ref PrivateSubnet1DR
   168вҶ’        - !Ref PrivateSubnet2DR
   169вҶ’      SecurityGroupIds:
   170вҶ’        - !Ref LambdaSecurityGroupDR
   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’    # Environment with cross-region KMS
   173вҶ’    Environment:
   174вҶ’      Variables:
   175вҶ’        DYNAMODB_TABLE: !Sub 'fee_exemption_failures_${Environment}'
   176вҶ’        MAIN_QUEUE_URL: !Ref MainQueueURLDR
   177вҶ’        KMS_KEY_ARN: !GetAtt FeeExemptionKMSKey.Arn  # Multi-region key
   178вҶ’        REGION_ROLE: STANDBY
   179вҶ’        SALT_PARAMETER: !Sub '/fee-exemption/${Environment}/salt'
   180вҶ’        CIRCUIT_BREAKER_TABLE: !Sub 'fee_exemption_circuit_breaker_${Environment}'
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’    # Explicit IAM Role
   183вҶ’    Role: !GetAtt DRReplayLambdaRole.Arn
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’    # X-Ray Tracing
   186вҶ’    TracingConfig:
   187вҶ’      Mode: Active
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’    Tags:
   190вҶ’      - Key: Environment
   191вҶ’        Value: !Ref Environment
   192вҶ’      - Key: Role
   193вҶ’        Value: DR-Standby
   194вҶ’
   195вҶ’DRReplayLambdaRole:
   196вҶ’  Type: AWS::IAM::Role
   197вҶ’  Condition: IsSecondaryRegion
   198вҶ’  Properties:
   199вҶ’    RoleName: !Sub 'fee-exemption-replay-dr-${Environment}'
   200вҶ’    AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
   201вҶ’      Version: '2012-10-17'
   202вҶ’      Statement:
   203вҶ’        - Effect: Allow
   204вҶ’          Principal:
   205вҶ’            Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
   206вҶ’          Action: sts:AssumeRole
   207вҶ’    Policies:
   208вҶ’      - PolicyName: DRReplayPolicy
   209вҶ’        PolicyDocument:
   210вҶ’          Version: '2012-10-17'
   211вҶ’          Statement:
   212вҶ’            # DynamoDB access (Global Table in us-east-1)
   213вҶ’            - Effect: Allow
   214вҶ’              Action:
   215вҶ’                - dynamodb:Query
   216вҶ’                - dynamodb:GetItem
   217вҶ’                - dynamodb:UpdateItem
   218вҶ’                - dynamodb:PutItem
   219вҶ’              Resource:
   220вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:${AWS::AccountId}:table/fee_exemption_failures_${Environment}'
   221вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:${AWS::AccountId}:table/fee_exemption_failures_${Environment}/index/*'
   222вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:${AWS::AccountId}:table/fee_exemption_circuit_breaker_${Environment}'
   223вҶ’
   224вҶ’            # KMS access for CPF decryption (multi-region key)
   225вҶ’            - Effect: Allow
   226вҶ’              Action:
   227вҶ’                - kms:Decrypt
   228вҶ’                - kms:Encrypt
   229вҶ’                - kms:GenerateDataKey
   230вҶ’              Resource:
   231вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:kms:sa-east-1:${AWS::AccountId}:key/*'
   232вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:${AWS::AccountId}:key/*'
   233вҶ’              Condition:
   234вҶ’                StringEquals:
   235вҶ’                  'kms:RequestAlias': !Sub 'alias/fee-exemption-${Environment}'
   236вҶ’
   237вҶ’            # SSM for salt retrieval (replicated parameter)
   238вҶ’            - Effect: Allow
   239вҶ’              Action:
   240вҶ’                - ssm:GetParameter
   241вҶ’              Resource:
   242вҶ’                - !Sub 'arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/fee-exemption/${Environment}/*'
   243вҶ’
   244вҶ’            # SQS for message replay
   245вҶ’            - Effect: Allow
   246вҶ’              Action:
   247вҶ’                - sqs:SendMessage
   248вҶ’              Resource:

   292вҶ’### Issue 3: Salt Storage Vulnerability (Security)
   293вҶ’
   294вҶ’**Problem**: V3 SSM salt without versioning; if modified, existing cpf_tokens become orphaned.
   295вҶ’
   296вҶ’**Solution**: Versioned salt format `v1:hash` with migration path.
   297вҶ’
   298вҶ’```typescript
   299вҶ’// Salt versioning implementation
   300вҶ’interface VersionedSalt {
   301вҶ’  version: number;
   302вҶ’  value: string;
   303вҶ’  createdAt: string;
   304вҶ’  status: 'ACTIVE' | 'DEPRECATED' | 'RETIRED';
   305вҶ’}
   306вҶ’
   307вҶ’interface CpfToken {
   308вҶ’  token: string;        // Full versioned token: "v1:sha256hash"
   309вҶ’  version: number;      // Extracted version for queries
   310вҶ’}
   311вҶ’
   312вҶ’const CURRENT_SALT_VERSION = 1;
   313вҶ’
   314вҶ’const getSaltVersions = async (): Promise<Map<number, VersionedSalt>> => {
   315вҶ’  const parameter = await ssmClient.send(new GetParameterCommand({
   316вҶ’    Name: `/fee-exemption/${process.env.ENVIRONMENT}/salts`,
   317вҶ’    WithDecryption: true
   318вҶ’  }));
   319вҶ’
   320вҶ’  // Format: {"versions": [{"version": 1, "value": "...", "status": "ACTIVE"}, ...]}
   321вҶ’  const salts = JSON.parse(parameter.Parameter!.Value!);
   322вҶ’  return new Map(salts.versions.map((s: VersionedSalt) => [s.version, s]));
   323вҶ’};
   324вҶ’
   325вҶ’const tokenizeCpf = async (cpf: string, saltVersion: number = CURRENT_SALT_VERSION): Promise<CpfToken> => {
   326вҶ’  const salts = await getSaltVersions();
   327вҶ’  const salt = salts.get(saltVersion);
   328вҶ’
   329вҶ’  if (!salt || salt.status === 'RETIRED') {
   330вҶ’    throw new Error(`Salt version ${saltVersion} not available`);
   331вҶ’  }
   332вҶ’
   333вҶ’  const hash = crypto
   334вҶ’    .createHmac('sha256', salt.value)
   335вҶ’    .update(cpf)
   336вҶ’    .digest('hex');
   337вҶ’
   338вҶ’  return {
   339вҶ’    token: `v${saltVersion}:${hash}`,
   340вҶ’    version: saltVersion
   341вҶ’  };
   342вҶ’};
   343вҶ’
   344вҶ’const lookupCpfToken = async (cpf: string): Promise<CpfToken[]> => {
   345вҶ’  // Look up across all active and deprecated salt versions
   346вҶ’  const salts = await getSaltVersions();
   347вҶ’  const tokens: CpfToken[] = [];
   348вҶ’
   349вҶ’  for (const [version, salt] of salts) {
   350вҶ’    if (salt.status !== 'RETIRED') {
   351вҶ’      const hash = crypto
   352вҶ’        .createHmac('sha256', salt.value)
   353вҶ’        .update(cpf)
   354вҶ’        .digest('hex');
   355вҶ’      tokens.push({ token: `v${version}:${hash}`, version });
   356вҶ’    }
   357вҶ’  }
   358вҶ’
   359вҶ’  return tokens;
   360вҶ’};
   361вҶ’
   362вҶ’// DynamoDB schema update - add salt_version attribute
   363вҶ’interface FailureRecord {
   364вҶ’  cpf_token: string;       // PK: "v1:abc123..."
   365вҶ’  salt_version: number;    // For query optimization
   366вҶ’  cpf_encrypted: string;
   367вҶ’  // ... other fields
   368вҶ’}
   369вҶ’```
   370вҶ’
   371вҶ’**SSM Parameter Format**:
   372вҶ’
   373вҶ’```json
   374вҶ’{
   375вҶ’  "versions": [
   376вҶ’    {
   377вҶ’      "version": 1,
   378вҶ’      "value": "base64-encoded-salt-here",
   379вҶ’      "createdAt": "2026-02-04T00:00:00Z",
   380вҶ’      "status": "ACTIVE"
   381вҶ’    }
   382вҶ’  ],
   383вҶ’  "currentVersion": 1
   384вҶ’}
   385вҶ’```
   386вҶ’
   387вҶ’**Salt Rotation Runbook (RB-011)**:
   388вҶ’
   389вҶ’```markdown
   390вҶ’## Salt Rotation Procedure
   391вҶ’
   392вҶ’### When to Rotate
   393вҶ’- Suspected salt compromise
   394вҶ’- Annual rotation policy (optional)
   395вҶ’- Compliance requirement
   396вҶ’
   397вҶ’### Steps
   398вҶ’
   399вҶ’1. **Add new salt version** (DO NOT remove old salt)
   400вҶ’   ```bash
   401вҶ’   # Get current salts
   402вҶ’   CURRENT=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /fee-exemption/prod/salts --with-decryption --query 'Parameter.Value' --output text)
   403вҶ’
   404вҶ’   # Add new version
   405вҶ’   NEW_SALT=$(openssl rand -base64 32)
   406вҶ’   NEW_VERSION=$(echo "$CURRENT" | jq '.currentVersion + 1')
   407вҶ’
   408вҶ’   UPDATED=$(echo "$CURRENT" | jq --arg salt "$NEW_SALT" --arg version "$NEW_VERSION" '
   409вҶ’     .versions += [{"version": ($version | tonumber), "value": $salt, "createdAt": (now | todate), "status": "ACTIVE"}] |
   410вҶ’     .currentVersion = ($version | tonumber)
   411вҶ’   ')
   412вҶ’
   413вҶ’   aws ssm put-parameter --name /fee-exemption/prod/salts --value "$UPDATED" --type SecureString --overwrite
   414вҶ’   ```
   415вҶ’
   416вҶ’2. **Deprecate old salt version**
   417вҶ’   ```bash
   418вҶ’   UPDATED=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /fee-exemption/prod/salts --with-decryption --query 'Parameter.Value' --output text | \
   419вҶ’     jq '.versions |= map(if .version == 1 then .status = "DEPRECATED" else . end)')
   420вҶ’   aws ssm put-parameter --name /fee-exemption/prod/salts --value "$UPDATED" --type SecureString --overwrite
   421вҶ’   ```
   422вҶ’
   423вҶ’3. **Retire old salt** (only after all records migrated or expired)
   424вҶ’   - Wait for TTL to expire all v1 records
   425вҶ’   - Verify no queries for v1 tokens in logs
   426вҶ’   - Update status to RETIRED
   427вҶ’
   428вҶ’### Rollback
   429вҶ’- If issues occur, revert SSM parameter to previous version
   430вҶ’- Old tokens remain valid as long as salt exists
   431вҶ’```
   432вҶ’
   433вҶ’### Issue 4: Circuit Breaker State Persistence (SRE)
   434вҶ’
   435вҶ’**Problem**: V3 Lambda is stateless; SSM has race conditions and latency for circuit breaker.
   436вҶ’
   437вҶ’**Solution**: DynamoDB-backed circuit breaker with atomic operations.
   438вҶ’
   439вҶ’```typescript
   440вҶ’// Circuit breaker state stored in dedicated DynamoDB table
   441вҶ’interface CircuitBreakerState {
   442вҶ’  service_id: string;           // PK: "CBILL"
   443вҶ’  state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN';
   444вҶ’  failure_count: number;
   445вҶ’  success_count: number;        // For HALF_OPEN recovery
   446вҶ’  last_failure_at: string | null;
   447вҶ’  last_success_at: string | null;
   448вҶ’  opened_at: string | null;
   449вҶ’  version: number;              // Optimistic locking
   450вҶ’  ttl: number;                  // Auto-reset after 1 hour
   451вҶ’}
   452вҶ’
   453вҶ’const CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG = {
   454вҶ’  failureThreshold: 5,          // Open after 5 consecutive failures
   455вҶ’  successThreshold: 3,          // Close after 3 consecutive successes in HALF_OPEN
   456вҶ’  openDurationMs: 60000,        // Stay open for 1 minute
   457вҶ’  halfOpenRequests: 3           // Allow 3 test requests in HALF_OPEN
   458вҶ’};
   459вҶ’
   460вҶ’class DynamoCircuitBreaker {
   461вҶ’  private tableName: string;
   462вҶ’  private serviceId: string;
   463вҶ’
   464вҶ’  constructor(tableName: string, serviceId: string) {
   465вҶ’    this.tableName = tableName;
   466вҶ’    this.serviceId = serviceId;
   467вҶ’  }
   468вҶ’
   469вҶ’  async getState(): Promise<CircuitBreakerState> {
   470вҶ’    const result = await dynamoClient.send(new GetCommand({
   471вҶ’      TableName: this.tableName,
   472вҶ’      Key: { service_id: this.serviceId }
   473вҶ’    }));
   474вҶ’
   475вҶ’    if (!result.Item) {
   476вҶ’      // Initialize with CLOSED state
   477вҶ’      return this.initializeState();
   478вҶ’    }
   479вҶ’
   480вҶ’    const state = result.Item as CircuitBreakerState;
   481вҶ’
   482вҶ’    // Check if OPEN state has expired
   483вҶ’    if (state.state === 'OPEN' && state.opened_at) {
   484вҶ’      const openedAt = new Date(state.opened_at).getTime();
   485вҶ’      if (Date.now() - openedAt >= CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG.openDurationMs) {
   486вҶ’        return this.transitionTo('HALF_OPEN', state);
   487вҶ’      }
   488вҶ’    }
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’    return state;
   491вҶ’  }
   492вҶ’
   493вҶ’  async recordSuccess(): Promise<CircuitBreakerState> {
   494вҶ’    const current = await this.getState();
   495вҶ’
   496вҶ’    if (current.state === 'CLOSED') {
   497вҶ’      return current; // No change needed
   498вҶ’    }
   499вҶ’
   500вҶ’    if (current.state === 'HALF_OPEN') {
   501вҶ’      const newSuccessCount = current.success_count + 1;
   502вҶ’      if (newSuccessCount >= CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG.successThreshold) {
   503вҶ’        return this.transitionTo('CLOSED', current);
   504вҶ’      }
   505вҶ’
   506вҶ’      // Increment success count
   507вҶ’      return this.updateSuccessCount(current, newSuccessCount);
   508вҶ’    }
   509вҶ’
   510вҶ’    return current;
   511вҶ’  }
   512вҶ’
   513вҶ’  async recordFailure(error: Error): Promise<CircuitBreakerState> {
   514вҶ’    const current = await this.getState();
   515вҶ’
   516вҶ’    if (current.state === 'OPEN') {
   517вҶ’      return current; // Already open, ignore
   518вҶ’    }
   519вҶ’
   520вҶ’    const newFailureCount = current.failure_count + 1;
   521вҶ’
   522вҶ’    if (current.state === 'HALF_OPEN' ||
   523вҶ’        newFailureCount >= CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG.failureThreshold) {
   524вҶ’      return this.transitionTo('OPEN', current);
   525вҶ’    }
   526вҶ’
   527вҶ’    return this.updateFailureCount(current, newFailureCount);
   528вҶ’  }
   529вҶ’
   530вҶ’  async canExecute(): Promise<boolean> {
   531вҶ’    const state = await this.getState();
   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’    if (state.state === 'CLOSED') return true;
   534вҶ’    if (state.state === 'OPEN') return false;
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’    // HALF_OPEN: allow limited requests
   537вҶ’    // Use atomic counter to limit concurrent test requests
   538вҶ’    try {
   539вҶ’      await dynamoClient.send(new UpdateCommand({
   540вҶ’        TableName: this.tableName,
   541вҶ’        Key: { service_id: this.serviceId },
   542вҶ’        UpdateExpression: 'SET half_open_requests = if_not_exists(half_open_requests, :zero) + :one',
   543вҶ’        ConditionExpression: 'half_open_requests < :max',
   544вҶ’        ExpressionAttributeValues: {
   545вҶ’          ':zero': 0,
   546вҶ’          ':one': 1,
   547вҶ’          ':max': CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG.halfOpenRequests
   548вҶ’        }
   549вҶ’      }));
   550вҶ’      return true;
   551вҶ’    } catch (err) {
   552вҶ’      if ((err as Error).name === 'ConditionalCheckFailedException') {
   553вҶ’        return false;
   554вҶ’      }
   555вҶ’      throw err;
   556вҶ’    }
   557вҶ’  }
   558вҶ’
   559вҶ’  private async transitionTo(
   560вҶ’    newState: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN',
   561вҶ’    current: CircuitBreakerState
   562вҶ’  ): Promise<CircuitBreakerState> {
   563вҶ’    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   564вҶ’
   565вҶ’    const update = await dynamoClient.send(new UpdateCommand({
   566вҶ’      TableName: this.tableName,
   567вҶ’      Key: { service_id: this.serviceId },
   568вҶ’      UpdateExpression: `
   569вҶ’        SET #state = :newState,
   570вҶ’            failure_count = :zero,
   571вҶ’            success_count = :zero,
   572вҶ’            half_open_requests = :zero,
   573вҶ’            opened_at = :openedAt,
   574вҶ’            last_transition_at = :now,
   575вҶ’            version = version + :one,
   576вҶ’            #ttl = :ttl
   577вҶ’      `,
   578вҶ’      ConditionExpression: 'version = :currentVersion',
   579вҶ’      ExpressionAttributeNames: {
   580вҶ’        '#state': 'state',
   581вҶ’        '#ttl': 'ttl'
   582вҶ’      },
   583вҶ’      ExpressionAttributeValues: {
   584вҶ’        ':newState': newState,
   585вҶ’        ':zero': 0,
   586вҶ’        ':openedAt': newState === 'OPEN' ? now : null,
   587вҶ’        ':now': now,
   588вҶ’        ':one': 1,
   589вҶ’        ':currentVersion': current.version,
   590вҶ’        ':ttl': Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 3600 // 1 hour TTL
   591вҶ’      },
   592вҶ’      ReturnValues: 'ALL_NEW'
   593вҶ’    }));
   594вҶ’
   595вҶ’    // Emit metric for state transition
   596вҶ’    await cloudwatch.send(new PutMetricDataCommand({
   597вҶ’      Namespace: 'FeeExemption/CircuitBreaker',
   598вҶ’      MetricData: [{
   599вҶ’        MetricName: 'StateTransition',
   600вҶ’        Dimensions: [
   601вҶ’          { Name: 'ServiceId', Value: this.serviceId },
   602вҶ’          { Name: 'FromState', Value: current.state },
   603вҶ’          { Name: 'ToState', Value: newState }
   604вҶ’        ],
   605вҶ’        Value: 1,
   606вҶ’        Unit: 'Count'
   607вҶ’      }]
   608вҶ’    }));
   609вҶ’
   610вҶ’    return update.Attributes as CircuitBreakerState;
   611вҶ’  }
   612вҶ’
   613вҶ’  private async initializeState(): Promise<CircuitBreakerState> {
   614вҶ’    const initialState: CircuitBreakerState = {
   615вҶ’      service_id: this.serviceId,
   616вҶ’      state: 'CLOSED',
   617вҶ’      failure_count: 0,
   618вҶ’      success_count: 0,
   619вҶ’      last_failure_at: null,
   620вҶ’      last_success_at: null,
   621вҶ’      opened_at: null,
   622вҶ’      version: 1,
   623вҶ’      ttl: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 3600
   624вҶ’    };
   625вҶ’
   626вҶ’    await dynamoClient.send(new PutCommand({
   627вҶ’      TableName: this.tableName,
   628вҶ’      Item: initialState,
   629вҶ’      ConditionExpression: 'attribute_not_exists(service_id)'
   630вҶ’    }));
   631вҶ’
   632вҶ’    return initialState;
   633вҶ’  }
   634вҶ’}
   635вҶ’```
   636вҶ’
   637вҶ’**CloudFormation for Circuit Breaker Table**:
   638вҶ’
   639вҶ’```yaml
   640вҶ’CircuitBreakerTable:
   641вҶ’  Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
   642вҶ’  Properties:
   643вҶ’    TableName: !Sub 'fee_exemption_circuit_breaker_${Environment}'
   644вҶ’    BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
   645вҶ’    AttributeDefinitions:
   646вҶ’      - AttributeName: service_id
   647вҶ’        AttributeType: S
   648вҶ’    KeySchema:
   649вҶ’      - AttributeName: service_id
   650вҶ’        KeyType: HASH
   651вҶ’    # NOTE: TTL removed per SRE review - circuit breaker state should persist
   652вҶ’    # indefinitely to prevent silent state reset after idle periods
   653вҶ’    PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
   654вҶ’      PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
   655вҶ’    # Encryption at rest with customer-managed KMS key

   657вҶ’      SSEEnabled: true
   658вҶ’      SSEType: KMS
   659вҶ’      KMSMasterKeyId: !Ref FeeExemptionKMSKey
   660вҶ’    Tags:
   661вҶ’      - Key: Application

   663вҶ’      - Key: Component
   664вҶ’        Value: circuit-breaker
   665вҶ’```
   666вҶ’
   667вҶ’### Issue 5: TTL Archiver Missing Failure Handling (SRE)
   668вҶ’
   669вҶ’**Problem**: V3 S3 write failure = permanent data loss.
   670вҶ’
   671вҶ’**Solution**: DLQ + retry + dead letter notification.
   672вҶ’
   673вҶ’```typescript
   674вҶ’// TTL Archiver with comprehensive error handling
   675вҶ’const archiveHandler = async (event: DynamoDBStreamEvent): Promise<SQSBatchResponse> => {
   676вҶ’  const batchItemFailures: SQSBatchItemFailure[] = [];
   677вҶ’
   678вҶ’  for (const record of event.Records) {
   679вҶ’    try {
   680вҶ’      if (record.eventName !== 'REMOVE' ||
   681вҶ’          record.userIdentity?.principalId !== 'dynamodb.amazonaws.com') {
   682вҶ’        continue;
   683вҶ’      }
   684вҶ’
   685вҶ’      const oldImage = unmarshall(record.dynamodb!.OldImage!);
   686вҶ’
   687вҶ’      // Validate required fields exist
   688вҶ’      if (!oldImage.cpf_token || !oldImage.cpf_encrypted) {
   689вҶ’        logger.error('Invalid record structure', { record_id: record.eventID });
   690вҶ’        // Don't retry invalid records, but log for investigation
   691вҶ’        await publishToDeadLetterTopic({
   692вҶ’          type: 'INVALID_RECORD_STRUCTURE',
   693вҶ’          record_id: record.eventID,
   694вҶ’          timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
   695вҶ’        });
   696вҶ’        continue;
   697вҶ’      }
   698вҶ’
   699вҶ’      const archiveRecord = {
   700вҶ’        cpf_token: oldImage.cpf_token,
   701вҶ’        cpf_encrypted: oldImage.cpf_encrypted,
   702вҶ’        created_at: oldImage.created_at,
   703вҶ’        resolved_at: oldImage.status === 'RESOLVED' ? oldImage.last_retry_at : null,
   704вҶ’        final_status: oldImage.status,
   705вҶ’        error_codes: [oldImage.error_code],
   706вҶ’        total_retry_count: oldImage.total_retry_count || 0,
   707вҶ’        archived_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   708вҶ’        archive_version: '1.0'
   709вҶ’      };
   710вҶ’
   711вҶ’      // Write to S3 with explicit error handling
   712вҶ’      await writeToS3WithRetry(archiveRecord, {
   713вҶ’        maxRetries: 3,
   714вҶ’        retryDelayMs: 1000
   715вҶ’      });
   716вҶ’
   717вҶ’      logger.info('Record archived successfully', {
   718вҶ’        cpf_token: archiveRecord.cpf_token,
   719вҶ’        created_at: archiveRecord.created_at
   720вҶ’      });
   721вҶ’
   722вҶ’    } catch (error) {
   723вҶ’      logger.error('Failed to archive record', {
   724вҶ’        error: (error as Error).message,
   725вҶ’        record_id: record.eventID
   726вҶ’      });
   727вҶ’
   728вҶ’      // Add to batch failures for SQS retry
   729вҶ’      batchItemFailures.push({
   730вҶ’        itemIdentifier: record.eventID!
   731вҶ’      });
   732вҶ’    }
   733вҶ’  }
   734вҶ’
   735вҶ’  return { batchItemFailures };
   736вҶ’};
   737вҶ’
   738вҶ’const writeToS3WithRetry = async (
   739вҶ’  record: ArchiveRecord,
   740вҶ’  options: { maxRetries: number; retryDelayMs: number }
   741вҶ’): Promise<void> => {
   742вҶ’  const key = generateArchiveKey(record);
   743вҶ’
   744вҶ’  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= options.maxRetries; attempt++) {
   745вҶ’    try {
   746вҶ’      await s3Client.send(new PutObjectCommand({
   747вҶ’        Bucket: process.env.ARCHIVE_BUCKET!,
   748вҶ’        Key: key,
   749вҶ’        Body: JSON.stringify(record),
   750вҶ’        ContentType: 'application/json',
   751вҶ’        ServerSideEncryption: 'aws:kms',
   752вҶ’        SSEKMSKeyId: process.env.KMS_KEY_ARN!,
   753вҶ’        Metadata: {
   754вҶ’          'cpf-token': record.cpf_token,
   755вҶ’          'archive-version': record.archive_version
   756вҶ’        }
   757вҶ’      }));
   758вҶ’      return;
   759вҶ’    } catch (error) {
   760вҶ’      if (attempt === options.maxRetries) {
   761вҶ’        throw error;
   762вҶ’      }
   763вҶ’      await sleep(options.retryDelayMs * attempt);
   764вҶ’    }
   765вҶ’  }
   766вҶ’};
   767вҶ’
   768вҶ’const generateArchiveKey = (record: ArchiveRecord): string => {
   769вҶ’  const date = new Date(record.created_at);
   770вҶ’  return `archives/year=${date.getFullYear()}/month=${String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')}/` +
   771вҶ’         `day=${String(date.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')}/${record.cpf_token}.json`;
   772вҶ’};
   773вҶ’
   774вҶ’const publishToDeadLetterTopic = async (failure: object): Promise<void> => {
   775вҶ’  await snsClient.send(new PublishCommand({
   776вҶ’    TopicArn: process.env.ARCHIVE_DLQ_TOPIC_ARN!,
   777вҶ’    Message: JSON.stringify(failure),
   778вҶ’    Subject: 'TTL Archiver Failure'
   779вҶ’  }));
   780вҶ’};
   781вҶ’```
   782вҶ’

   819вҶ’### Issue 6: Durability SLO Impractical (SRE)
   820вҶ’
   821вҶ’**Problem**: V3's 99.999% durability at ~1000 writes/month means 1 lost record exhausts annual budget.
   822вҶ’
   823вҶ’**Solution**: Revised SLOs aligned with actual volume and risk tolerance.
   824вҶ’
   825вҶ’```yaml
   826вҶ’# Revised SLO Framework
   827вҶ’
   828вҶ’| SLO | Target | Rationale |
   829вҶ’|-----|--------|-----------|
   830вҶ’| **Availability** | 99.9% | Unchanged - reasonable for internal service |
   831вҶ’| **Data Durability** | 99.9% | Realistic: 1 lost record per 1000 (1/month budget) |
   832вҶ’| **Replay Latency** | p95 < 10 min | Relaxed from 5 min - adequate for 48h resolution SLA |
   833вҶ’| **Time to Resolution** | < 48 hours | Unchanged |
   834вҶ’| **Successful Replay Rate** | > 95% | Relaxed from 98% - accounts for CBILL issues |
   835вҶ’
   836вҶ’# Error Budget Calculations (Monthly)
   837вҶ’
   838вҶ’| SLO | Budget | Practical Meaning |
   839вҶ’|-----|--------|-------------------|
   840вҶ’| Availability | 43.8 min downtime | ~2 short outages |
   841вҶ’| Durability | 1 lost record | Acceptable given archive fallback |
   842вҶ’| Replay Latency | 5% > 10 min | ~50 slow replays |
   843вҶ’| Resolution | 5% > 48h | ~50 delayed resolutions |
   844вҶ’| Replay Success | 5% failures | ~50 failed replays |
   845вҶ’```
   846вҶ’
   847вҶ’### Issue 7: Global Tables Over-Engineering (Architecture)
   848вҶ’
   849вҶ’**Problem**: Global Tables cost $1.25/month + 2x writes for 500 records/month with 48h SLA.
   850вҶ’
   851вҶ’**Solution**: Single-region with S3 cross-region backup (simpler, cheaper, adequate).
   852вҶ’
   853вҶ’**Decision Matrix**:
   854вҶ’
   855вҶ’| Factor | Global Tables | S3 Cross-Region Backup |
   856вҶ’|--------|---------------|------------------------|
   857вҶ’| RPO | < 1 minute | < 15 minutes |
   858вҶ’| RTO | < 5 minutes | < 30 minutes |
   859вҶ’| Cost | ~$1.25/month extra | ~$0.10/month extra |
   860вҶ’| Complexity | Higher (multi-region writes) | Lower (read replicas only) |
   861вҶ’| Processing Continuity | Yes (if CBILL multi-region) | Data preservation only |
   862вҶ’
   863вҶ’**Recommendation**: **Remove Global Tables** unless:
   864вҶ’1. Compliance mandates multi-region data residency, OR
   865вҶ’2. CBILL has multi-region endpoints
   866вҶ’

   905вҶ’### S3 Backup Lambda Specification (Critical - Missing in V3)
   906вҶ’
   907вҶ’**Purpose**: Export DynamoDB changes to S3 for cross-region replication.
   908вҶ’
   909вҶ’**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES)
   910вҶ’
   911вҶ’```typescript
   912вҶ’// S3 Backup Lambda - Idempotent export to S3
   913вҶ’interface BackupRecord {
   914вҶ’  cpf_token: string;
   915вҶ’  salt_version: number;
   916вҶ’  cpf_encrypted: string;
   917вҶ’  created_at: string;
   918вҶ’  status: string;
   919вҶ’  error_code: string;
   920вҶ’  version: number;  // DynamoDB item version for idempotency
   921вҶ’  exported_at: string;
   922вҶ’}
   923вҶ’
   924вҶ’const backupHandler = async (event: DynamoDBStreamEvent): Promise<StreamsEventResponse> => {
   925вҶ’  const batchItemFailures: { itemIdentifier: string }[] = [];
   926вҶ’
   927вҶ’  for (const record of event.Records) {
   928вҶ’    try {
   929вҶ’      // Skip DELETE events (handled by TTL Archiver)
   930вҶ’      if (record.eventName === 'REMOVE') continue;
   931вҶ’
   932вҶ’      const newImage = record.dynamodb?.NewImage
   933вҶ’        ? unmarshall(record.dynamodb.NewImage)
   934вҶ’        : null;
   935вҶ’
   936вҶ’      if (!newImage) continue;
   937вҶ’
   938вҶ’      const backupRecord: BackupRecord = {
   939вҶ’        cpf_token: newImage.cpf_token,
   940вҶ’        salt_version: newImage.salt_version,
   941вҶ’        cpf_encrypted: newImage.cpf_encrypted,
   942вҶ’        created_at: newImage.created_at,
   943вҶ’        status: newImage.status,
   944вҶ’        error_code: newImage.error_code,
   945вҶ’        version: newImage.version,
   946вҶ’        exported_at: new Date().toISOString()
   947вҶ’      };
   948вҶ’
   949вҶ’      // Idempotent S3 key: includes version to prevent overwrites
   950вҶ’      const s3Key = generateIdempotentKey(backupRecord);
   951вҶ’
   952вҶ’      await s3Client.send(new PutObjectCommand({
   953вҶ’        Bucket: process.env.BACKUP_BUCKET!,
   954вҶ’        Key: s3Key,
   955вҶ’        Body: JSON.stringify(backupRecord),
   956вҶ’        ContentType: 'application/x-ndjson',
   957вҶ’        ServerSideEncryption: 'aws:kms',
   958вҶ’        SSEKMSKeyId: process.env.KMS_KEY_ARN!
   959вҶ’      }));
   960вҶ’
   961вҶ’      logger.info('Record backed up', {
   962вҶ’        cpf_token: backupRecord.cpf_token,
   963вҶ’        version: backupRecord.version
   964вҶ’      });
   965вҶ’
   966вҶ’    } catch (error) {
   967вҶ’      logger.error('Backup failed', {
   968вҶ’        error: (error as Error).message,
   969вҶ’        eventID: record.eventID
   970вҶ’      });
   971вҶ’      batchItemFailures.push({ itemIdentifier: record.eventID! });
   972вҶ’    }
   973вҶ’  }
   974вҶ’
   975вҶ’  return { batchItemFailures };
   976вҶ’};
   977вҶ’
   978вҶ’const generateIdempotentKey = (record: BackupRecord): string => {
   979вҶ’  const date = new Date(record.created_at);
   980вҶ’  // Key format: backups/year=YYYY/month=MM/day=DD/{cpf_token}_{version}.json
   981вҶ’  return `backups/year=${date.getFullYear()}/month=${String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')}/` +
   982вҶ’         `day=${String(date.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')}/${record.cpf_token}_v${record.version}.json`;
   983вҶ’};
   984вҶ’```
   985вҶ’

  1058вҶ’**DR Restore Procedure** (RB-013):
  1059вҶ’
  1060вҶ’```markdown
  1061вҶ’## DR Restore from S3 Backup
  1062вҶ’
  1063вҶ’### Trigger
  1064вҶ’- Primary region DynamoDB unavailable
  1065вҶ’- Data corruption requiring point-in-time recovery
  1066вҶ’
  1067вҶ’### Steps
  1068вҶ’
  1069вҶ’1. **Create new DynamoDB table in DR region**
  1070вҶ’   ```bash
  1071вҶ’   aws dynamodb create-table \
  1072вҶ’     --table-name fee_exemption_failures_dr_restore \
  1073вҶ’     --attribute-definitions file://schema.json \
  1074вҶ’     --region us-east-1
  1075вҶ’   ```
  1076вҶ’
  1077вҶ’2. **List available backups**
  1078вҶ’   ```bash
  1079вҶ’   aws s3 ls s3://fee-exemption-backup-${ACCOUNT}-prod-dr/backups/ --recursive | tail -100
  1080вҶ’   ```
  1081вҶ’
  1082вҶ’3. **Import from S3 to DynamoDB**
  1083вҶ’   ```bash
  1084вҶ’   # Using AWS Data Pipeline or custom Lambda
  1085вҶ’   aws lambda invoke \
  1086вҶ’     --function-name fee-exemption-dr-restore \
  1087вҶ’     --payload '{"source_prefix": "backups/year=2026/month=02/"}' \
  1088вҶ’     response.json
  1089вҶ’   ```
  1090вҶ’
  1091вҶ’4. **Verify record count**
  1092вҶ’   ```bash
  1093вҶ’   aws dynamodb scan --table-name fee_exemption_failures_dr_restore --select COUNT
  1094вҶ’   ```
  1095вҶ’
  1096вҶ’5. **Update application configuration to use restored table**
  1097вҶ’```
  1098вҶ’
  1099вҶ’### Issue 8: WAF Disproportionate (Architecture)
  1100вҶ’
  1101вҶ’**Problem**: $5.50/month WAF for ~200 internal requests; private API reduces need.
  1102вҶ’
  1103вҶ’**Solution**: Remove WAF for private VPC-only API. Rely on:
  1104вҶ’- VPC endpoint with security group restrictions
  1105вҶ’- API Gateway resource policies
  1106вҶ’- IAM authentication
  1107вҶ’- Rate limiting at API Gateway level
  1108вҶ’
  1109вҶ’```yaml
  1110вҶ’# API Gateway without WAF (private API)
  1111вҶ’FeeExemptionAPI:
  1112вҶ’  Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
  1113вҶ’  Properties:
  1114вҶ’    Name: !Sub 'fee-exemption-api-${Environment}'
  1115вҶ’    EndpointConfiguration:
  1116вҶ’      Types:
  1117вҶ’        - PRIVATE
  1118вҶ’      VpcEndpointIds:
  1119вҶ’        - !Ref APIGatewayVPCEndpoint
  1120вҶ’
  1121вҶ’    # Resource policy restricts to VPC
  1122вҶ’    Policy:
  1123вҶ’      Version: '2012-10-17'
  1124вҶ’      Statement:
  1125вҶ’        - Effect: Deny
  1126вҶ’          Principal: '*'
  1127вҶ’          Action: execute-api:Invoke
  1128вҶ’          Resource: !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*/*/*/*'
  1129вҶ’          Condition:
  1130вҶ’            StringNotEquals:
  1131вҶ’              'aws:SourceVpce': !Ref APIGatewayVPCEndpoint
  1132вҶ’        - Effect: Allow
  1133вҶ’          Principal: '*'
  1134вҶ’          Action: execute-api:Invoke
  1135вҶ’          Resource: !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*/*/*/*'
  1136вҶ’
  1137вҶ’# Rate limiting via usage plan
  1138вҶ’APIUsagePlan:

  1145вҶ’    Quota:

  1190вҶ’## Health Check Lambda Specification
  1191вҶ’
  1192вҶ’```typescript
  1193вҶ’interface HealthCheckResponse {
  1194вҶ’  status: 'healthy' | 'degraded' | 'unhealthy';
  1195вҶ’  timestamp: string;
  1196вҶ’  components: {
  1197вҶ’    dynamodb: ComponentHealth;
  1198вҶ’    sqs: ComponentHealth;
  1199вҶ’    cbill: ComponentHealth;
  1200вҶ’    circuitBreaker: CircuitBreakerHealth;
  1201вҶ’  };
  1202вҶ’  metrics: {
  1203вҶ’    pendingRetryCount: number;
  1204вҶ’    inProgressCount: number;
  1205вҶ’    lastHourFailures: number;
  1206вҶ’  };
  1207вҶ’}
  1208вҶ’
  1209вҶ’interface ComponentHealth {
  1210вҶ’  status: 'healthy' | 'unhealthy';
  1211вҶ’  latencyMs: number;
  1212вҶ’  message?: string;
  1213вҶ’}
  1214вҶ’
  1215вҶ’interface CircuitBreakerHealth {
  1216вҶ’  status: 'healthy' | 'warning' | 'critical';
  1217вҶ’  state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN';
  1218вҶ’  failureCount: number;
  1219вҶ’}
  1220вҶ’
  1221вҶ’const healthCheckHandler = async (): Promise<APIGatewayProxyResult> => {
  1222вҶ’  const startTime = Date.now();
  1223вҶ’  const components: HealthCheckResponse['components'] = {
  1224вҶ’    dynamodb: await checkDynamoDB(),
  1225вҶ’    sqs: await checkSQS(),
  1226вҶ’    cbill: await checkCBILL(),
  1227вҶ’    circuitBreaker: await checkCircuitBreaker()
  1228вҶ’  };
  1229вҶ’
  1230вҶ’  const metrics = await getHealthMetrics();
  1231вҶ’
  1232вҶ’  const overallStatus = determineOverallStatus(components, metrics);
  1233вҶ’
  1234вҶ’  const response: HealthCheckResponse = {
  1235вҶ’    status: overallStatus,
  1236вҶ’    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  1237вҶ’    components,
  1238вҶ’    metrics
  1239вҶ’  };
  1240вҶ’
  1241вҶ’  return {
  1242вҶ’    statusCode: overallStatus === 'healthy' ? 200 : overallStatus === 'degraded' ? 200 : 503,
  1243вҶ’    body: JSON.stringify(response),
  1244вҶ’    headers: {
  1245вҶ’      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  1246вҶ’      'Cache-Control': 'no-cache'
  1247вҶ’    }
  1248вҶ’  };
  1249вҶ’};
  1250вҶ’
  1251вҶ’const checkDynamoDB = async (): Promise<ComponentHealth> => {
  1252вҶ’  const start = Date.now();
  1253вҶ’  try {
  1254вҶ’    await dynamoClient.send(new DescribeTableCommand({
  1255вҶ’      TableName: process.env.DYNAMODB_TABLE!
  1256вҶ’    }));
  1257вҶ’    return {
  1258вҶ’      status: 'healthy',
  1259вҶ’      latencyMs: Date.now() - start
  1260вҶ’    };
  1261вҶ’  } catch (error) {
  1262вҶ’    return {
  1263вҶ’      status: 'unhealthy',
  1264вҶ’      latencyMs: Date.now() - start,
  1265вҶ’      message: (error as Error).message
  1266вҶ’    };
  1267вҶ’  }
  1268вҶ’};
  1269вҶ’
  1270вҶ’const checkSQS = async (): Promise<ComponentHealth> => {
  1271вҶ’  const start = Date.now();
  1272вҶ’  try {
  1273вҶ’    await sqsClient.send(new GetQueueAttributesCommand({
  1274вҶ’      QueueUrl: process.env.MAIN_QUEUE_URL!,
  1275вҶ’      AttributeNames: ['ApproximateNumberOfMessages']
  1276вҶ’    }));
  1277вҶ’    return {
  1278вҶ’      status: 'healthy',
  1279вҶ’      latencyMs: Date.now() - start
  1280вҶ’    };
  1281вҶ’  } catch (error) {
  1282вҶ’    return {
  1283вҶ’      status: 'unhealthy',
  1284вҶ’      latencyMs: Date.now() - start,
  1285вҶ’      message: (error as Error).message
  1286вҶ’    };
  1287вҶ’  }
  1288вҶ’};
  1289вҶ’
  1290вҶ’const checkCBILL = async (): Promise<ComponentHealth> => {
  1291вҶ’  const start = Date.now();
  1292вҶ’  // Lightweight GraphQL introspection query
  1293вҶ’  try {
  1294вҶ’    const response = await graphqlClient.query({
  1295вҶ’      query: '{ __typename }'
  1296вҶ’    });
  1297вҶ’    return {
  1298вҶ’      status: 'healthy',
  1299вҶ’      latencyMs: Date.now() - start
  1300вҶ’    };
  1301вҶ’  } catch (error) {
  1302вҶ’    return {
  1303вҶ’      status: 'unhealthy',
  1304вҶ’      latencyMs: Date.now() - start,
  1305вҶ’      message: (error as Error).message
  1306вҶ’    };
  1307вҶ’  }
  1308вҶ’};
  1309вҶ’
  1310вҶ’const checkCircuitBreaker = async (): Promise<CircuitBreakerHealth> => {
  1311вҶ’  const cb = new DynamoCircuitBreaker(
  1312вҶ’    process.env.CIRCUIT_BREAKER_TABLE!,
  1313вҶ’    'CBILL'
  1314вҶ’  );
  1315вҶ’  const state = await cb.getState();
  1316вҶ’
  1317вҶ’  return {
  1318вҶ’    status: state.state === 'CLOSED' ? 'healthy' :
  1319вҶ’            state.state === 'HALF_OPEN' ? 'warning' : 'critical',
  1320вҶ’    state: state.state,
  1321вҶ’    failureCount: state.failure_count
  1322вҶ’  };
  1323вҶ’};
  1324вҶ’
  1325вҶ’const getHealthMetrics = async (): Promise<HealthCheckResponse['metrics']> => {
  1326вҶ’  // Query DynamoDB for current counts
  1327вҶ’  const [pendingResult, inProgressResult] = await Promise.all([
  1328вҶ’    dynamoClient.send(new QueryCommand({
  1329вҶ’      TableName: process.env.DYNAMODB_TABLE!,
  1330вҶ’      IndexName: 'status-created_at-index',
  1331вҶ’      KeyConditionExpression: 'status_shard = :status',
  1332вҶ’      ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':status': 'PENDING_RETRY#0' },
  1333вҶ’      Select: 'COUNT'
  1334вҶ’    })),
  1335вҶ’    dynamoClient.send(new QueryCommand({
  1336вҶ’      TableName: process.env.DYNAMODB_TABLE!,
  1337вҶ’      IndexName: 'status-created_at-index',
  1338вҶ’      KeyConditionExpression: 'status_shard = :status',
  1339вҶ’      ExpressionAttributeValues: { ':status': 'IN_PROGRESS#0' },
  1340вҶ’      Select: 'COUNT'
  1341вҶ’    }))
  1342вҶ’  ]);
  1343вҶ’
  1344вҶ’  return {
  1345вҶ’    pendingRetryCount: pendingResult.Count || 0,
  1346вҶ’    inProgressCount: inProgressResult.Count || 0,
  1347вҶ’    lastHourFailures: await getLastHourFailureCount()
  1348вҶ’  };
  1349вҶ’};
  1350вҶ’```

  1459вҶ’## Decision Log (Updated)
  1460вҶ’
  1461вҶ’| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered |
  1462вҶ’|------|----------|-----------|------------------------|
  1463вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | Multi-region KMS over regional | DR without key sync complexity | Create new key per region |
  1464вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | DynamoDB circuit breaker over SSM | Atomic operations, no race conditions | SSM (simpler but races), Redis (overkill) |
  1465вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | S3 backup over Global Tables | Simpler, cheaper, adequate RPO for 48h SLA | Global Tables (more complex) |
  1466вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | Remove WAF for private API | Cost/benefit ratio poor for internal API | Keep WAF (unnecessary cost) |
  1467вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | Versioned salt format | Enables rotation without data loss | Single salt (rotation = data loss) |
  1468вҶ’| 2026-02-04 | Relaxed durability SLO | 99.999% impractical at low volume | Keep 99.999% (unrealistic) |

1вҶ’# Solution V5: CPF Queue Automation for Fee Exemption Reprocessing
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’> Architecture Design Document - Revision 6 (Architecture-Focused)
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Revision History
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
     8вҶ’|---------|------|--------|---------|
     9вҶ’| V0 | 2026-02-03 | Initial | Original proposal |
    10вҶ’| V1 | 2026-02-03 | REV002 | Detailed architecture |
    11вҶ’| V2 | 2026-02-04 | REV002 | Security, SRE, DevOps fixes |
    12вҶ’| V3 | 2026-02-04 | REV003 | Production readiness, DR, SLOs, Testing |
    13вҶ’| V4 | 2026-02-04 | REV004 | Production hardening, critical issue fixes |
    14вҶ’| V5 | 2026-02-04 | REV005 | Architecture-focused: removed implementation details |
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’---
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’## Executive Summary
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’This document describes the **architecture solution** for automating the reprocessing of failed fee exemption postings. V5 is a refinement of V4 that **removes implementation-level details** (code, IAM policies, CloudFormation) to focus on architectural decisions. Implementation details will be addressed in the development phase.
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’### Problem Statement
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’When fee exemption promotions fail integration with CBILL (on-premises financial system), the current process requires:
    25вҶ’1. Manual identification of failed CPFs from DLQ
    26вҶ’2. Opening a ticket to the CBILL team for reprocessing
    27вҶ’3. No visibility into failure patterns or root causes
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’**Constraints**:
    30вҶ’- DLQ has 14-day TTL - messages lost after this period
    31вҶ’- CBILL is on-premises with limited availability guarantees
    32вҶ’- CPF is PII requiring LGPD-compliant handling
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’### Solution Overview
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’Introduce a **persistent failure store** with **selective replay capability** and **automated retry scheduling**, eliminating manual intervention for common failure scenarios.
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’---
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’## Architecture Diagram
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’```

    44вҶ’в”Ӯ                              CURRENT FLOW                                        в”Ӯ

   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’## Core Components
   117вҶ’
   118вҶ’### 1. DLQ-Consumer Lambda
   119вҶ’
   120вҶ’**Purpose**: Consume messages from DLQ and persist to DynamoDB failure store.
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’**Trigger**: SQS Event Source Mapping from `SQS-IsenГ§ГЈo_mensalidade_DLQ`
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’**Key Behaviors**:
   125вҶ’- Tokenizes CPF using SHA-256 + salt (LGPD compliance)
   126вҶ’- Encrypts original CPF with KMS before storage
   127вҶ’- Performs atomic writes with conditional expressions to prevent duplicates
   128вҶ’- Reports batch item failures for partial batch handling
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’**Interactions**:
   131вҶ’- Reads from: SQS DLQ
   132вҶ’- Writes to: DynamoDB Failure Store
   133вҶ’- Uses: KMS (encryption), SSM (salt retrieval)
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’### 2. DynamoDB Failure Store
   136вҶ’
   137вҶ’**Purpose**: Persistent store for failed messages, queryable by CPF or status.
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’**Table Design**:
   140вҶ’
   141вҶ’| Attribute | Type | Description |
   142вҶ’|-----------|------|-------------|
   143вҶ’| `cpf_token` (PK) | String | SHA-256 hash of CPF with salt prefix `v{N}:hash` |
   144вҶ’| `created_at` (SK) | String | ISO timestamp for multiple failures per CPF |
   145вҶ’| `cpf_encrypted` | Binary | KMS-encrypted original CPF |
   146вҶ’| `status` | String | PENDING_RETRY, IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, FAILED_PERMANENT |
   147вҶ’| `status_shard` | String | `{status}#{shard}` for GSI partitioning |
   148вҶ’| `error_code` | String | Original error from CBILL |
   149вҶ’| `error_message` | String | Detailed error description |
   150вҶ’| `retry_count` | Number | Number of automated retries |
   151вҶ’| `next_retry_at` | String | Scheduled next retry timestamp |
   152вҶ’| `version` | Number | Optimistic locking version |
   153вҶ’| `ttl` | Number | Auto-deletion after 90 days |
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’**Global Secondary Index (GSI)**:
   156вҶ’- Name: `status-created_at-index`
   157вҶ’- PK: `status_shard`
   158вҶ’- SK: `created_at`
   159вҶ’- Purpose: Query records by status for batch replay
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’**Design Decisions**:
   162вҶ’- **Salt-versioned tokens** (`v1:hash`) enable salt rotation without orphaning records
   163вҶ’- **Sharded status key** prevents GSI hot partitions
   164вҶ’- **TTL for auto-cleanup** with archive before deletion
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’### 3. Replay Lambda
   167вҶ’
   168вҶ’**Purpose**: Re-inject failed messages back to the main processing queue.
   169вҶ’
   170вҶ’**Triggers**:
   171вҶ’1. **Scheduled**: EventBridge rule every 15 minutes
   172вҶ’2. **Manual**: API Gateway endpoint for selective replay
   173вҶ’3. **Stream**: DynamoDB Streams for immediate retry of specific patterns
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’**Key Behaviors**:
   176вҶ’- Queries DynamoDB for PENDING_RETRY records with `next_retry_at < now()`
   177вҶ’- Checks circuit breaker state before processing
   178вҶ’- Decrypts CPF using KMS
   179вҶ’- Sends message to main SQS queue
   180вҶ’- Updates record status to IN_PROGRESS with optimistic locking
   181вҶ’- Implements exponential backoff for retry scheduling
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’**Interactions**:
   184вҶ’- Reads from: DynamoDB Failure Store, Circuit Breaker Table
   185вҶ’- Writes to: SQS Main Queue, DynamoDB (status updates)
   186вҶ’- Uses: KMS (decryption)
   187вҶ’
   188вҶ’### 4. Circuit Breaker
   189вҶ’
   190вҶ’**Purpose**: Protect system from cascading failures when CBILL is unavailable.
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’**State Machine**:
   193вҶ’```
   194вҶ’           5 consecutive          1 min timeout
   195вҶ’           failures               (configurable)
   196вҶ’    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   197вҶ’    в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   198вҶ’    в–ј                 в”Ӯ      в–ј                 в”Ӯ
   199вҶ’ CLOSED в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә OPEN в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә HALF_OPEN
   200вҶ’    в–І                               в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
   201вҶ’    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
   202вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ 3 successes в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в”Ӯ
   203вҶ’              in HALF_OPEN               в”Ӯ
   204вҶ’                                         в”Ӯ
   205вҶ’    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ 1 failure в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   206вҶ’    в–ј
   207вҶ’  OPEN
   208вҶ’```
   209вҶ’
   210вҶ’**Storage**: Dedicated DynamoDB table (not SSM) for:
   211вҶ’- Atomic state transitions via conditional writes
   212вҶ’- No race conditions between Lambda invocations
   213вҶ’- Persistent state (no TTL to prevent silent reset)
   214вҶ’- Encryption at rest with KMS
   215вҶ’
   216вҶ’**States**:
   217вҶ’- **CLOSED**: Normal operation, all requests allowed
   218вҶ’- **OPEN**: CBILL unavailable, all replay requests blocked
   219вҶ’- **HALF_OPEN**: Testing recovery, limited requests allowed
   220вҶ’
   221вҶ’### 5. Stuck Recovery Lambda
   222вҶ’
   223вҶ’**Purpose**: Detect and reset records stuck in IN_PROGRESS state.
   224вҶ’
   225вҶ’**Trigger**: EventBridge schedule every 15 minutes
   226вҶ’
   227вҶ’**Key Behaviors**:
   228вҶ’- Queries GSI for IN_PROGRESS records older than 30 minutes
   229вҶ’- Resets status to PENDING_RETRY
   230вҶ’- Increments retry count
   231вҶ’- Emits CloudWatch metric for monitoring
   232вҶ’
   233вҶ’### 6. TTL Archiver Lambda
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’**Purpose**: Archive records before TTL deletion for LGPD compliance (6-year retention).
   236вҶ’
   237вҶ’**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (REMOVE events only)
   238вҶ’
   239вҶ’**Key Behaviors**:
   240вҶ’- Filters for TTL-initiated deletions only
   241вҶ’- Writes record to S3 with Hive-partitioned keys
   242вҶ’- Implements retry with DLQ for write failures
   243вҶ’- Maintains audit trail for compliance
   244вҶ’
   245вҶ’**S3 Key Format**: `archives/year={Y}/month={M}/day={D}/{cpf_token}.json`
   246вҶ’
   247вҶ’### 7. S3 Backup Lambda
   248вҶ’
   249вҶ’**Purpose**: Real-time backup to S3 for disaster recovery.
   250вҶ’
   251вҶ’**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (INSERT and MODIFY events)
   252вҶ’
   253вҶ’**Key Behaviors**:
   254вҶ’- Exports new/modified records to S3 in near real-time
   255вҶ’- Uses version-based keys for idempotency: `backups/{date}/{cpf_token}_v{version}.json`
   256вҶ’- Enables cross-region replication for DR
   257вҶ’- Does not process DELETE events (handled by TTL Archiver)
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’### 8. Health Check Lambda
   260вҶ’
   261вҶ’**Purpose**: Provide system health status for monitoring and alerting.
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’**Trigger**: API Gateway endpoint, CloudWatch Synthetic Canary
   264вҶ’
   265вҶ’**Components Checked**:
   266вҶ’- DynamoDB table accessibility
   267вҶ’- SQS queue availability
   268вҶ’- CBILL connectivity (lightweight probe)
   269вҶ’- Circuit breaker state
   270вҶ’
   271вҶ’**Response Format**:
   272вҶ’```json
   273вҶ’{
   274вҶ’  "status": "healthy|degraded|unhealthy",
   275вҶ’  "components": {
   276вҶ’    "dynamodb": {"status": "healthy", "latencyMs": 15},
   277вҶ’    "sqs": {"status": "healthy", "latencyMs": 8},
   278вҶ’    "cbill": {"status": "unhealthy", "latencyMs": 5000},
   279вҶ’    "circuitBreaker": {"state": "OPEN", "failureCount": 5}
   280вҶ’  },
   281вҶ’  "metrics": {
   282вҶ’    "pendingRetryCount": 42,
   283вҶ’    "inProgressCount": 3,
   284вҶ’    "lastHourFailures": 12
   285вҶ’  }
   286вҶ’}
   287вҶ’```
   288вҶ’
   289вҶ’### 9. Private API Gateway
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’**Purpose**: Manual replay interface for Squad FГӘnix.
   292вҶ’
   293вҶ’**Endpoints**:
   294вҶ’
   295вҶ’| Method | Path | Description |
   296вҶ’|--------|------|-------------|
   297вҶ’| GET | `/v1/failures` | List failures with pagination and filters |
   298вҶ’| GET | `/v1/failures/{cpf}` | Get failures for specific CPF |
   299вҶ’| POST | `/v1/replay` | Trigger replay for specific CPF(s) or all pending |
   300вҶ’| GET | `/v1/health` | System health check |
   301вҶ’
   302вҶ’**Security**:
   303вҶ’- Private API (VPC endpoint only)
   304вҶ’- IAM authentication
   305вҶ’- Resource policy restricting to VPC
   306вҶ’- Rate limiting via usage plans
   307вҶ’
   308вҶ’---
   309вҶ’
   310вҶ’## Data Flow Patterns
   311вҶ’
   312вҶ’### Pattern 1: Automated Retry (Happy Path)
   313вҶ’
   314вҶ’```
   315вҶ’1. Message fails in FeeExemption Lambda after X retries
   316вҶ’2. Message lands in DLQ
   317вҶ’3. DLQ-Consumer persists to DynamoDB with status=PENDING_RETRY, next_retry_at=now+15min
   318вҶ’4. EventBridge triggers Replay Lambda
   319вҶ’5. Replay Lambda queries PENDING_RETRY records where next_retry_at < now
   320вҶ’6. Circuit breaker check passes (CLOSED state)
   321вҶ’7. CPF decrypted, message sent to main queue
   322вҶ’8. Status updated to IN_PROGRESS
   323вҶ’9. FeeExemption processes successfully
   324вҶ’10. (External) Success callback updates status to RESOLVED
   325вҶ’```
   326вҶ’
   327вҶ’### Pattern 2: Circuit Breaker Protection
   328вҶ’
   329вҶ’```
   330вҶ’1. CBILL becomes unavailable
   331вҶ’2. FeeExemption failures accumulate (5+ consecutive)
   332вҶ’3. Circuit breaker transitions to OPEN
   333вҶ’4. Replay Lambda skips processing, emits metric
   334вҶ’5. After 1 minute, circuit breaker transitions to HALF_OPEN
   335вҶ’6. Limited test requests allowed
   336вҶ’7. 3 consecutive successes вҶ’ CLOSED
   337вҶ’8. OR 1 failure вҶ’ OPEN again
   338вҶ’```
   339вҶ’
   340вҶ’### Pattern 3: Manual Selective Replay
   341вҶ’
   342вҶ’```
   343вҶ’1. Operator identifies specific CPF(s) to replay
   344вҶ’2. POST /v1/replay with cpf list
   345вҶ’3. Replay Lambda bypasses schedule, processes immediately
   346вҶ’4. Circuit breaker still enforced (can be overridden in emergency)
   347вҶ’5. Response includes success/failure for each CPF
   348вҶ’```
   349вҶ’
   350вҶ’### Pattern 4: Data Lifecycle
   351вҶ’
   352вҶ’```
   353вҶ’Day 0:   Record created (status=PENDING_RETRY)
   354вҶ’Day 1-7: Automated retries with exponential backoff
   355вҶ’Day 7+:  Manual intervention if still failing
   356вҶ’Day 90:  TTL triggers deletion
   357вҶ’         вҶ’ DynamoDB Stream вҶ’ TTL Archiver вҶ’ S3 Archive
   358вҶ’Year 6:  S3 Glacier Deep Archive auto-expires (LGPD compliance)
   359вҶ’```
   360вҶ’
   361вҶ’---
   362вҶ’
   363вҶ’## Security Architecture
   364вҶ’
   365вҶ’### CPF Protection (LGPD Compliance)
   366вҶ’
   367вҶ’**Tokenization Strategy**:
   368вҶ’- CPF never stored in plaintext
   369вҶ’- SHA-256 HMAC with rotating salt creates `cpf_token`
   370вҶ’- Salt versioning (`v1:hash`) enables rotation without data loss
   371вҶ’- Original CPF encrypted with KMS before storage
   372вҶ’
   373вҶ’**Key Management**:
   374вҶ’- Dedicated KMS key for CPF encryption
   375вҶ’- Multi-region key for DR scenarios
   376вҶ’- Key rotation enabled (annual)
   377вҶ’- MFA required for key administration
   378вҶ’
   379вҶ’### Network Security
   380вҶ’
   381вҶ’- All Lambdas run in VPC (same as FeeExemption)
   382вҶ’- Private API Gateway (VPC endpoint only)
   383вҶ’- No public internet exposure
   384вҶ’- Security groups restrict egress to necessary endpoints only
   385вҶ’
   386вҶ’### Access Control
   387вҶ’
   388вҶ’- IAM roles with least-privilege per Lambda
   389вҶ’- No wildcard permissions in production
   390вҶ’- Cross-region IAM for DR Lambdas explicitly defined
   391вҶ’- API Gateway authentication via IAM
   392вҶ’
   393вҶ’---
   394вҶ’
   395вҶ’## Disaster Recovery
   396вҶ’
   397вҶ’### Strategy: Data Durability (Not Processing Continuity)
   398вҶ’
   399вҶ’**Rationale**: CBILL is on-premises in a single location. If sa-east-1 fails, CBILL is likely also affected. DR focus is on **preserving data** until primary region recovers.
   400вҶ’
   401вҶ’### Components
   402вҶ’
   403вҶ’1. **S3 Cross-Region Replication**
   404вҶ’   - Source: `fee-exemption-backup-{account}-{env}` (sa-east-1)
   405вҶ’   - Target: `fee-exemption-backup-{account}-{env}-dr` (us-east-1)
   406вҶ’   - RPO: < 15 minutes (replication SLA)
   407вҶ’   - RTO: < 30 minutes (restore procedure)
   408вҶ’
   409вҶ’2. **Multi-Region KMS Key**
   410вҶ’   - Single logical key, replicated across regions
   411вҶ’   - DR Lambdas can decrypt without key synchronization
   412вҶ’
   413вҶ’3. **SSM Parameter Replication**
   414вҶ’   - Salt values replicated to DR region
   415вҶ’   - EventBridge rule triggers replication on change
   416вҶ’
   417вҶ’### What DR Does NOT Include
   418вҶ’
   419вҶ’- Active processing in secondary region (requires multi-region CBILL)
   420вҶ’- Global Tables (cost/complexity disproportionate for 48h SLA)
   421вҶ’- Active-active architecture
   422вҶ’
   423вҶ’### Restore Procedure (High-Level)
   424вҶ’
   425вҶ’1. Create new DynamoDB table in DR region
   426вҶ’2. Import from S3 backup (Lambda or AWS Data Pipeline)
   427вҶ’3. Update application configuration to use restored table
   428вҶ’4. Resume processing when CBILL available
   429вҶ’
   430вҶ’---
   431вҶ’
   432вҶ’## Observability
   433вҶ’
   434вҶ’### CloudWatch Alarms
   435вҶ’
   436вҶ’| Alarm | Threshold | Action |
   437вҶ’|-------|-----------|--------|
   438вҶ’| DLQ-Consumer Errors | > 5 in 5 min | Alert Squad FГӘnix |
   439вҶ’| Replay Lambda Errors | > 10 in 15 min | Alert + check CBILL |
   440вҶ’| Circuit Breaker OPEN | State = OPEN | Page on-call |
   441вҶ’| Stuck IN_PROGRESS Records | > 10 for 1 hour | Alert for investigation |
   442вҶ’| TTL Archiver DLQ Messages | > 0 | Critical - potential data loss |
   443вҶ’| DynamoDB Throttling | > 0 | Scale or optimize |
   444вҶ’
   445вҶ’### CloudWatch Dashboard
   446вҶ’
   447вҶ’Sections:
   448вҶ’1. **Queue Health**: DLQ depth, main queue age
   449вҶ’2. **Failure Store**: Records by status, daily trend
   450вҶ’3. **Replay Metrics**: Success rate, latency p95
   451вҶ’4. **Circuit Breaker**: Current state, transition history
   452вҶ’5. **System Health**: Lambda errors, throttles, duration
   453вҶ’
   454вҶ’### Logging
   455вҶ’
   456вҶ’- Structured JSON format
   457вҶ’- CPF masked in logs (show last 4 digits only)
   458вҶ’- Trace ID propagation (X-Ray)
   459вҶ’- Correlation ID for cross-service tracking
   460вҶ’
   461вҶ’---
   462вҶ’
   463вҶ’## SLOs and Error Budgets
   464вҶ’
   465вҶ’| SLO | Target | Error Budget (Monthly) |
   466вҶ’|-----|--------|------------------------|
   467вҶ’| Availability | 99.9% | 43.8 min downtime |
   468вҶ’| Data Durability | 99.9% | 1 lost record per 1000 |
   469вҶ’| Replay Latency | p95 < 10 min | 5% slow replays |
   470вҶ’| Time to Resolution | < 48 hours | 5% exceed SLA |
   471вҶ’| Successful Replay Rate | > 95% | 5% permanent failures |
   472вҶ’
   473вҶ’---

   480вҶ’|-----------|----------------|

   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’| ID | Runbook | Trigger |

   548вҶ’
   549вҶ’## Decision Log
   550вҶ’
   551вҶ’| Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Rejected |
   552вҶ’|----------|-----------|----------------------|
   553вҶ’| DynamoDB over RDS | Serverless, pay-per-use, simple KV pattern | RDS (over-provisioned), Redis (no durability) |
   554вҶ’| SHA-256 tokenization | Industry standard, irreversible | Base64 (reversible), bcrypt (slower) |
   555вҶ’| Salt versioning | Enables rotation without data loss | Single salt (rotation = orphaned records) |
   556вҶ’| DynamoDB circuit breaker | Atomic operations, persistent | SSM (race conditions), in-memory (stateless) |
   557вҶ’| S3 backup over Global Tables | Simpler, cheaper, adequate RPO for 48h SLA | Global Tables (2x cost, unnecessary complexity) |
   558вҶ’| Private API over public | Internal use only, security | Public + WAF (unnecessary cost) |
   559вҶ’| Remove WAF | Private API in VPC, low traffic | Keep WAF ($5.50/month for 200 requests) |
   560вҶ’| 99.9% durability SLO | Realistic for ~500 records/month | 99.999% (1 record exhausts annual budget) |
   561вҶ’

    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’## SumГЎrio Executivo
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’Este documento descreve a **soluГ§ГЈo arquitetural** para automatizar o reprocessamento de postagens de isenГ§ГЈo de mensalidade que falharam. A V6 Г© uma traduГ§ГЈo da V5 para portuguГӘs brasileiro, mantendo foco em decisГөes arquiteturais. Detalhes de implementaГ§ГЈo (cГіdigo, polГӯticas IAM, CloudFormation) serГЈo abordados na fase de desenvolvimento.
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### DeclaraГ§ГЈo do Problema
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’Quando promoГ§Гөes de isenГ§ГЈo de mensalidade falham na integraГ§ГЈo com o CBILL (sistema financeiro on-premises), o processo atual requer:
    26вҶ’1. IdentificaГ§ГЈo manual de CPFs falhos na DLQ
    27вҶ’2. Abertura de chamado para a equipe do CBILL reprocessar
    28вҶ’3. Sem visibilidade sobre padrГөes de falha ou causas-raiz
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’**RestriГ§Гөes**:
    31вҶ’- DLQ possui TTL de 14 dias - mensagens sГЈo perdidas apГіs esse perГӯodo
    32вҶ’- CBILL Г© on-premises com garantias limitadas de disponibilidade
    33вҶ’- CPF Г© dado pessoal sensГӯvel, requer tratamento conforme LGPD
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’### VisГЈo Geral da SoluГ§ГЈo
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’Introduzir um **armazenamento persistente de falhas** com **capacidade de replay seletivo** e **agendamento automГЎtico de retry**, eliminando intervenГ§ГЈo manual para cenГЎrios comuns de falha.
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’---
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’## Diagrama de Arquitetura
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’```
    44вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

    47вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                                  в”Ӯ

    55вҶ’в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ
